title: internal/config: YAML config parser — Load/Save, v1 format, gqlc init interface
description:
Design locked in grill session 2026-07-11/12. CLI-agnostic package internal/config using gopkg.in/yaml.v3 (promote to direct dep).

PUBLIC API:
- const DefaultFilename = "gqlc.yaml" (canonical name only; no .yml/.json variants; no search logic)
- Load(path string) (Config, error); Decode(r io.Reader) (Config, error) (procsig parity)
- (Config) Save(path string) error — canonical YAML for future 'gqlc init', golden-fixture pinned, round-trips through Load
- Typed enums, single source of truth for decode+errors+prompts: SchemaLang (SchemaLangGQLC="gqlc"), QueryLang (QueryLangOpenCypher="opencypher"), Driver (DriverNeo4jGoV5="neo4j-go-v5") + XxxValues() accessors
- Config struct = canonical LATEST shape, NO Version field (version is wire-only): SchemaPath, QueryDir, OutputDir, OutputPackage, ProcsigPath (optional, empty=absent), SchemaLang, QueryLang, Driver

WIRE FORMAT v1 (snake_case keys, declaration order = canonical Save order): version(int, required, only 1), schema, queries, output, package (all required non-empty), schema_language, query_language, driver (required enums), procsig (optional, explicit "" rejected as ambiguous).

LOAD SEMANTICS: version probe (lenient two-field decode, pointer int: nil=missing-field error, !=1 = 'declares version N; only version 1 is supported') then dispatch to strict v1 decoder — the seam for future v2 (per-version decoders normalizing into one Config; NO version interface). Strict decode: yaml.Decoder KnownFields(true), wireV1 with pointer fields for precise missing-field errors listing valid enum values. Enum UnmarshalYAML rejects invalid values with line info: 'line N: invalid query_language "x" (valid values: opencypher)'. package must pass go/token.IsIdentifier. NO filesystem checks (relative paths are relative to the config file's dir — documented semantics, future CLI resolves). Empty file gets actionable error (procsig precedent).

ERRORS: no custom sentinels; fmt.Errorf with 'config: <path>: ...' prefix, %w wrapping (fs.ErrNotExist passes through for future gqlc init exists-check). Tests assert substrings, procsig table style.

DELIVERABLES: internal/config + tests + testdata golden fixture; docs/specs/config-file-format.md (field table, error catalogue, rationale: CLI-agnostic, required single-value enums, seam-not-interface versioning); CONTEXT.md glossary entry (already drafted, committed on branch).

Precedent: internal/procsig/file.go + docs/specs/procsig-file-format.md. Refs gqlc-aqb (stays open — config carries the procsig path; threading into cypher.WithRegistry is aqb's scope once the CLI exists).
close_reason: Shipped in PR #258 (squash 8ce98ee). internal/config: Load/Decode/Save v1, tag-strict version probe, typed single-value enums with XxxValues() vocabulary, KnownFields strict decode, golden fixture + round-trip, spec doc docs/specs/config-file-format.md, CONTEXT.md glossary entry. Linus round 1 FAIL (1 BLOCKER: yaml float→int truncation in version probe; 5 NITs) → fixes in 1aa535c → round 2 verified PASS. Two non-blocking NITs deferred to follow-up bead.

title: Nested golden module: add golangci-lint to test-codegen-fence
description: The C0 spec §7 pins 'go build && go vet' inside test/data/codegen as the compile fence. Owner clarified 2026-07-11 that generated code must uphold the same linting + formatting standards as gqlc's own CI. To enforce: add a .golangci.yml at test/data/codegen (matching or a subset of the root .golangci.yml), extend 'just test-codegen-fence' recipe to also run 'golangci-lint run' inside the nested module, and update the CI job codegen-fence to match. Filed as a follow-up in case C0 code cycle lands the recipe without the linter arm; the C0 review will grill for this and this bead can close if C0 already includes it.
close_reason: Folded into PR #224 (master 4ff49ac). The 'just test-codegen-fence' recipe was extended in commit e3f0736 to run 'golangci-lint run' inside the nested module (discovers root .golangci.yml via upward walk — parity for free, no duplicate config to maintain) plus 'go mod tidy -diff' (catches the // indirect drift Linus flagged, nit #1). CI job codegen-fence inherits. Generated goldens verified: 0 lint issues at C0. No standalone bead needed.

title: Driver wiring: runtime layer for generated repositories
description:
Post-freeze runtime integration for the code-generated repository layer. The generator's output (see gqlc-8i0) needs a driver/runtime layer — connection handling, query dispatch, result decoding into the typed Go values — that the generated code calls into. Per ADR 0004 §Decision, written once against the frozen model.

Split off from #14 / gqlc-0mx after the resolver portion completed (R0-R7 shipped 2026-07-05 through 2026-07-06). Driver wiring is a distinct concern from both the resolver and the codegen.
close_reason: Closed outright per C6 spec §8: all five original scope items (session opening, per-method access dispatch, entity decode helpers, WithTx, driver-native routing) fully subsumed by C0-C5 inline emissions. No standalone runtime layer needed per ADR 0010 D4. Auto-closed via PR #244 'Closes #150'.

title: Codegen: type-safe repository generator from ValidatedQuery
description:
Post-freeze code generator that takes a ValidatedQuery (produced by internal/resolver) plus a schema and emits type-safe Go repository code. Per ADR 0004 §Decision, written once against the frozen query.Query / ValidatedQuery model.

Split off from #14 / gqlc-0mx after the resolver portion completed — R0-R7 all shipped 2026-07-05 through 2026-07-06 (see gqlc-0mx.2 through gqlc-0mx.9). Codegen is a separate concern from the resolver and gets its own tracking. The generated driver wiring is tracked separately again.
notes: ORCHESTRATION (for any agent driving this epic, 2026-07-11): Work the dotted children gqlc-8i0.2 (C0) -> .8 (C6) strictly in order — they are dependency-chained, so 'bd ready' surfaces exactly the one unblocked stage. Per stage: claim the bead, read docs/adr/0010-codegen-typed-repositories-from-validatedquery.md in full (stage scope = D7; cross-cutting = D1-D6/D8) + the bead's own notes, then spec-first (docs/specs/codegen-stage-c*.md, own PR) before any code, then golden tests, then implementation — each in its own worktree+branch, adversarial implement->review loop, verified reviewer PASS authorizes squash-merge. One stage per fresh session/team (context budget). Close each bead with PR # in the reason and close its GH mirror. Post-v1 satellites (not in the chain): gqlc-1a5 (:iter), gqlc-v5t (resolver builtins); gqlc-0aa re-scoped at C6. Epic closes when .8 closes — final PR or manual gh close for GH #151 (umbrella: child PRs must NOT write 'Closes #151').
close_reason: Codegen epic COMPLETE. All six stages C0-C6 delivered: PR #224 (C0 harness), #228 (C1 reads), #231 (C2 entities), #234 (C3 collections/temporals/widths), #237 (C4 writes), #241 (C5 edgeUnion), #244 (C6 polish + epic close). GH umbrella #151 auto-closed by PR #244. Post-v1 satellites: gqlc-1a5 (:iter), gqlc-v5t (resolver builtins), gqlc-73h (testcontainers). Docs-only: gqlc-2ud (C4 §4.9 amendment). Autonomous ultraloop 2026-07-10→2026-07-11: Carmack+Linus subagent pattern with Linus PASS = merge auth; owner asleep throughout; all decisions agent-gated.

Sibling of gqlc-ude (read surface, Stages 1-5, complete). gqlc-cta dependencies extended to each piece.

Stages 6-14 (gqlc-97z, 9b4, 9nl, ydk, 2av, 665, mkv, cqh, im1) + scope ADR gqlc-39s + TCK sweep gqlc-x6u + pre-freeze fixes gqlc-33k.1/.2 + gqlc-3r0 all closed; freeze gqlc-cta landed as PR #81 (6bdb86c, ADR 0008). title: Stage 14: CALL + procedure signature registry
description: New compile-time input artifact: procedure signatures, declared alongside the schema (TCK precedent step: there exists a procedure test.my.proc(in :: INTEGER?) :: (out :: INTEGER?)). Type CALL...YIELD, YIELD *, and standalone CALL - the latter two are untypeable without the signature, so an unreferenced/unknown procedure is a generation-time compile error (a feature: fails at codegen instead of runtime). Needs godog step defs that build the per-scenario registry. Wire TCK dir: clauses/call (~41 scenario outlines).
close_reason: Closed

title: Stage 13: MERGE + ON CREATE / ON MATCH
description: MERGE upsert semantics on top of the Stage 12 write model: match-or-create pattern binding plus conditional SET branches (ON CREATE SET / ON MATCH SET). Both branches contribute to parameter typing. Wire TCK dir: clauses/merge (~75 scenario outlines). Depends on Stage 12.
close_reason: Closed

title: Stage 12: write clauses - CREATE, DELETE, SET, REMOVE
description: The headline scope expansion: query.Query gains a read/write statement distinction (drivers need transaction mode), a write-effect shape, and projection-less statements (no RETURN). SET value typing against the schema yields parameter type inference for writes - the core of the repository-pattern goal (typed Create/Update/Delete methods). DETACH DELETE included. TCK side-effect assertion steps (the side effects should be) are reinterpreted parse-level per gqlc-39s. Wire TCK dirs: clauses/{create,delete,set,remove} (~205 scenario outlines). Depends on Stage 6 (SET/CREATE property expressions).
close_reason: Merged as PR #67 (0971f42), GH #43 closed. StatementKind axis + Part.Effects (7 variants), 6th sentinel ErrNestedPropertyTarget, SF3 write-golden harness. Suite 3740/3307/433/0, goldens 3061, pins 88/15. Follow-ups filed: gqlc-33k.3, gqlc-33k.4, gqlc-mpn.

title: Stage 11: predicate expressions - quantifiers + existential subqueries
description: ALL/ANY/NONE/SINGLE list quantifiers and EXISTS {} existential subqueries (patterns inside expressions - scoping rules matter: inner pattern variables must not leak into the outer scope). Result type is always BOOLEAN so model impact is thin; grammar and scoping are the work. Wire TCK dirs: expressions/{quantifier,existentialSubqueries} (~110 scenario outlines). Depends on Stage 6.
close_reason: Stage 11 merged: PR #65 (45045fe), GH #44 closed. Quantifiers ALL/ANY/NONE/SINGLE + EXISTS{} subqueries, TypeBool. Two review rounds (round-1 FAIL: mineComparisons descent leak → guard fix cffa32e + 4 red pins). Counts: suite 3535/3099/436/0; pins 75/13; sentinels 5. Follow-up gqlc-33k.3 filed (EXISTS LIMIT/SKIP param imprecision).

title: ADR: expand pre-freeze scope to full openCypher surface (writes, CALL, expressions)
description: Product goal (owner, 2026-07-01): gqlc must generate type-safe, compile-safe repository implementations for practically ANY openCypher query - reads AND writes. ADR 0004 planned Stages 1-5 (read core) then freeze; that is now insufficient: query.Query must also model writes, MERGE, CALL, full patterns, and the full expression/type surface BEFORE the freeze (gqlc-cta), because retrofitting them after codegen exists would be far more expensive. Write a new ADR documenting: (a) the three TCK buckets - must-model (writes/merge/call/patterns/read clauses), must-parse-with-thin-model-impact (most expressions: only the result type matters, semantics pass through per ADR 0005), out-of-scope (runtime-error scenarios; result/side-effect assertions are reinterpreted at parse level); (b) explicit reversal of the permanent read-core skiplist exclusions (named paths x161, variable-length x59, multi-type x2, rich projections x53, writes x9) - those were deliberate Stage-0-5 scope cuts, now in scope; (c) procedure signatures as a new compile-time input artifact alongside the schema (TCK precedent: 'there exists a procedure test.my.proc(in :: INTEGER?) :: (out :: INTEGER?)'); (d) stage renumbering 6-14 with per-stage specs in docs/specs/ as before. Type vocabulary completeness (lists, maps, temporal, path) is part of the frozen interface and must be settled here.

title: Stage 1 — non-property-typed parameters (SKIP/LIMIT $p)
description: First model evolution after Stage 0: parameters whose type comes from their slot rather than a binding property. Specifically bare $p in SKIP and LIMIT. Introduces query.Use as a sealed sum and relaxes invariant D4.
design:
Unlock bare SKIP $p / LIMIT $p as non-property-typed parameter uses.

Model change:
- Introduce sealed sum `query.Use` = PropertyUse | PositionalUse (mirrors Binding/Endpoint discipline)
- PropertyUse wraps a Ref (existing case)
- PositionalUse carries a `Position` enum (PositionSkip | PositionLimit), int-backed, stringer (mirrors graph.EntityKind)
- `Parameter.Uses` type changes from []Ref to []Use
- Invariant D4 relaxes: every Use is exactly one of PropertyUse | PositionalUse
- Smart constructors enforce invariants
- Tagged-union MarshalJSON with lowercase "kind": PropertyUse flattens to {"kind":"property","variable":...,"property":...}; PositionalUse to {"kind":"positional","position":"skip"|"limit"}

Accept/reject delta from Stage 0:
- SKIP $p (bare): reject → accept as PositionalUse{Skip}
- LIMIT $p (bare): reject → accept as PositionalUse{Limit}
- SKIP $p + 1, LIMIT f($p), ORDER BY $p, every other deferred-parameter construct: stay rejected
- Accept rule mirrors WHERE D1 directness discipline: bare positional only

Dedup unchanged: Parameter dedup by Name in first-appearance order. Mixed-kind uses on one Parameter are fine; the resolver judges type unification post-freeze (parser stays schema-agnostic per ADR 0003).

JSON casing wart: Parameter struct stays uppercase Go-default while Uses items go lowercase tagged-union. Acknowledged scope-creep-avoidance — defer harmonization to post-freeze.
acceptance_criteria:
- TCK scenarios in clauses/return-skip-limit/ exercising SKIP $p / LIMIT $p flip red→green
- Layer 2 targeted sentinel tests cover still-rejected cases (SKIP $p+1, ORDER BY $p)
- No previously-passing scenario regresses; pending count only decreases
- Both gated-swarm reviewers (Linus + Sandi) approve the diff
- `just test` green
notes:
Plan agreed via /grill-with-docs on 2026-05-26.

Reference docs:
- ADR 0004 — test-first, evolving-until-feature-complete (governs staged approach)
- ADR 0003 — schema-agnostic parser; resolver handles type unification
- ADR 0005 — generated code executes original text; model is type-interface only
- docs/specs/cypher-query-parser-stage-0.md — Stage 0 spec (canonical baseline)

Deliverables in the same PR as the code:
- docs/specs/cypher-query-parser-stage-1.md — short delta-style spec doc (~80-120 lines); structure mirrors Stage 0
- CONTEXT.md gains a "Use" entry; "Parameter" refined to reference it
- Stage 0 spec §4 C2 erratum: one-line note clarifying that label-conflict against the schema is the resolver's responsibility (the conjunctive constraint interpretation is faithful to openCypher, not a parser limitation)

TCK dir widening: add clauses/return-skip-limit/ to the godog dir list. Expect 1-2 skiplist additions to surface during implementation (runtime-error assertions we cannot model).

Implementation methodology: /tdd inside /gated-swarm (Carmack implements; Linus + Sandi review).

title: chore(ci): add live-smoke to master's required status checks
description:
gqlc-rvw (PR #323) landed the CODE half of promoting codegen-live to
PR-blocking — the workflow now runs on every PR. But the CONFIG half is
missing: master's branch-protection required_status_checks.contexts is
still [lint, test, tidy, actionlint, govulncheck]. \`live-smoke\` (the
codegen-live workflow's job name) is NOT in the required list, so it
runs but doesn't block auto-merge.

Proof (PR #323 itself): the live-smoke job was still IN_PROGRESS when
auto-merge fired at 05:37:22Z. Job completed successfully at 05:37:32Z
(10s later) — but had it failed, the merge would have happened anyway.
Wall-time on the runner was 63s (setup-go + setup-just + docker + both
arms in parallel).

Fix (one-line via the GH API):

  gh api -X PATCH \\
    repos/areqag/gqlc/branches/master/protection/required_status_checks \\
    -f 'contexts[]=lint' -f 'contexts[]=test' -f 'contexts[]=tidy' \\
    -f 'contexts[]=actionlint' -f 'contexts[]=govulncheck' \\
    -f 'contexts[]=live-smoke'

Verify: after apply, \`gh api repos/areqag/gqlc/branches/master/protection\`
should include \`live-smoke\` in contexts. Then any PR whose live-smoke
job FAILS will block auto-merge.

Alternative: do this via the GitHub UI at
https://github.com/areqag/gqlc/settings/branches — edit the master rule,
add \`live-smoke\` to required status checks.

This is a shared-infrastructure config change, not a code change — needs
owner action (or explicit owner sign-off before an agent runs the API
call).

Descends from: gqlc-rvw (which shipped the code half in PR #323).

title: master-guard claude-pre-bash hook false-positives on worktree commits
description: The PreToolUse master-guard hook (.githooks/claude-pre-bash:31) resolves the branch via 'git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD' in the hook's own pwd (the Bash tool's persistent cwd), not the git command's actual target. A 'git -C <worktree> commit' (or a command that cd's inside the worktree within the same call) targeting a feature branch is denied when the session cwd sits on master. Observed 2026-07-14 during gqlc-ls8.2 (commit-5 halt in .claude/worktrees/ls8-2-cypher). Workaround: persistently cd the Bash tool into the worktree so the hook resolves the right branch. Fix direction: make the hook resolve the branch at the command's effective target — parse 'git -C <path>' and leading 'cd <path> &&' from tool_input.command before rev-parse. Needs its own PR + review; do NOT patch mid-loop.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #310 (squash 6a83f0e). PreToolUse master-guard rewritten as a python3 analyzer: effective-target resolution (cd/pushd chains, -C joins), quote-aware heredoc stripping, substitution-body recursion, shlex tokenization kills prose false positives; symbolic-ref fallback covers unborn HEADs. 29-case harness wired into just test (CI parity). Adversarial Linus review: PASS after 2 SHOULD-FIXes applied.

comments.author: antranig-yeretzian
comments.text: Second finding (2026-07-14, same ls8.2 halt): the hook also has a FALSE NEGATIVE. Regex at line 25 requires the word git immediately followed by the word commit, so the form [git -C <path> commit ...] does not match at all and is never branch-checked — this is how the ls8-2 worktree commits (c4deb99..6f2b2c1) landed before the halt. Empirically verified: the bare form and the cd-compound form MATCH (then branch-check the hook pwd -> false positive for worktree work); the -C form passes unchecked. Also noted: the hook matches the raw command string including quoted literals and prose (a for-loop echoing the bare form as a string was denied; so was a bd-comment command whose prose contained it). Fix should: (1) parse -C / leading-cd to resolve the command's effective target dir, (2) branch-check THAT dir, (3) ideally ignore quoted literals. Interim team ruling for ls8.2: worktree commits proceed via the -C form — target branch is cypher-collection-sink, the hook's stated intent (block commits on master/main) is preserved, and repo git hooks (core.hooksPath pre-commit gates) still fire normally. This interim allowance dies when this bead's fix lands.

title: cypher-collection-sink spec §5 phase-ordering fix (Rev 3)
description: Rev 3 (9843af7) hoists the addParameterUse sink gate from Rev 2's Phase D bundle into a new Phase B.5 that MUST land before any Phase C handler migration. Rev 2 (452c6b7) shipped a phase-ordering bug: its §5 bundled l.fail and addParameterUse into Phase D (post-migration), but every Phase-C handler (Match/With/Set/Delete/Remove) has a reach path into addParameterUse via mineWhere/collectSetItem/typeExpressionMining. Dropping the Match guard during Phase C surfaced the leak: TestMustParse/exists_body_mixed_limit_skip_and_where_params (inline require.Equal, no -update path) went red because $threshold picked up a spurious PropertyUse via EnterOC_Match → mineWhere → pairAddSub → addParameterUse. Fix shipped as three commits: b5e206d (Phase B.5 sink gate, observably-nil at then-current stack tip), 764dcd8 (Match migration re-run under the gate, now green), 9843af7 (spec Rev 3: revision-history stanza with failure narrative, §3.4 approved-map correction, §5 restructure with Phase B/B.5/D). Note: an earlier attempt to formalise this fix committed as b126e87 with a misleading subject and a stanza that falsely claimed Rev 2 had already contained the fix; that commit was amended into 9843af7 after linus-2 grill against git receipts (git show 452c6b7 verifies Rev 2 shipped bundled Phase D, not Phase B.5). Close as part of the eventual chore(beads) close-out PR for gqlc-ls8.2, do NOT create a separate close-out PR just for it.

title: cli: extract the generate pipeline (config file -> []File) from runGenerate
description: internal/cli/generate.go (334 lines). title: codegen: prepare commits every render decision; render stops re-deriving resolver types
description: internal/codegen/{prepare.go (847), types.go, render_queries.go (853), render_models.go}. The prepare/render seam exists in name (preparedQuery) but decisions leak across it: prepare switches on resolver.ResolvedType (18 cases), types.go carries 24 more, and render_queries.go re-switches at emission time (10 cases, :592-663) plus references resolver.StatementWrite directly (:294). The EdgeKey canonical-order discipline between prepare.go and render_queries.go is coupled by comment (spec §5.5), not structure. Failure mode: a new ResolvedType variant compiles through default: arms and renders silently wrong generated code — the scariest failure class in the repo since generated output is the product (ADR 0010).
design: Deepen prepare: it commits every rendering decision (column kind, Go type string, decode strategy, required imports) into preparedQuery; render_*.go walks prepared data only and never touches a resolver type again — ADR 0010's 'load-bearing table' (D3) gets one home, and an unhandled ResolvedType becomes one loud sentinel in prepare instead of silent wrong output. Team decides the concrete preparedQuery shape in a docs/specs/ design doc, grilled by Linus BEFORE code. Generate's exported API and the []File contract (ADR 0010 D4) must not move.

Nested-module compile fence (go build && go vet in test/data/codegen) green. just test / fmt-check / lint green. render_*.go contains zero type-switches on resolver.ResolvedType and zero references to resolver enums (grep-verifiable). title: cypher parser: one collection sink replaces 14 per-handler subqueryDepth guards
description: internal/query/cypher/listener.go (+expr.go, pattern.go, call.go). The invariant 'bindings/refs/effects inside EXISTS { ... } never enter the outer query part' is enforced by 14 separate 'if l.subqueryDepth > 0' guards (listener.go:237,254,273,294,369,394,494,531,556,576,589,604,621,678) — every present and future Enter* handler must remember the guard, and a forgotten one produces a phantom binding the resolver types with the wrong scope (the exact failure ADR 0003's Stage-11 note names). ADR 0008's amendment history shows the cost: the 33k.3 EXISTS-param precision fix needed three sweep passes plus an idempotence guard.
design: Deepen the mutable listener state into one collection-sink module (a scope stack): handlers write bindings/returns/effects/params unconditionally; the sink routes to the outer part or discards, so suppression is decided at one seam instead of fourteen call sites. This preserves ADR 0003 Stage-11's invariant and its chosen posture (structural enforcement, not name-collision rules) — it concentrates enforcement, it does not re-litigate. Team decides the concrete interface in a docs/specs/ design doc, grilled by Linus BEFORE code. Wire shape must not move.
acceptance_criteria: All 3,199 parser goldens byte-identical (no -update). TCK acceptance suite: same pass/pending counts, zero failed. title: resolver: deepen the carried scope — branchState becomes a scope module
description: internal/resolver/resolve.go (1,977 lines). The carried scope — what a query part knows about each binding across a WITH — is one concept scattered over ten parallel maps in branchState (resolve.go:88: exportedNodeTypes, exportedEdgeTypes, exportedEdgeKeys, exportedEdgeCands, exportedEdgeBindings, exportedNullableBinding, exportedOptionalGroup, exportedResolvedTypes, exportedCallTypes, exportedOrder). Internal helper interfaces are as wide as the state they thread: exportScope (:524) takes 12 parameters; projectionType (:1181) and refProjectionType (:1206) take 10 each; demoteNullableInPlace (:1427) mutates the nullable map as a buried side effect. Deletion test fails: removing any one map requires edits at 20+ call sites. The filed ay9 close-out residual (cross-Part carry is name-granular, does not carry group ids) needs exactly a branchState extension — expensive under this shape, trivial after.
design: Deepen the scope into its own module (likely its own file, possibly own type with methods): one type owning the per-variable resolved facts behind lookup / carry-forward / demote operations, so helpers take one scope value and the ten maps become private implementation. The demotion fixed-point becomes a method testable directly. Team decides the concrete interface in a docs/specs/ design doc, grilled by Linus BEFORE code. Pure internal refactor: resolver.New / Resolve / ValidatedQuery are pinned by ADR 0008 and must not move.

title: CLI-2: gqlc init — interactive huh wizard for gqlc.yaml
description: Implement gqlc init with charm.land/huh/v2 (ADR 0011; dep enters go.mod here). TTY-only: guard with term.IsTerminal, fail cleanly otherwise (no flag matrix in v1); wire huh accessible mode (ACCESSIBLE env). Flows: (1) no config at target path (default ./gqlc.yaml, -f/--file to override): fresh wizard from sensible defaults; (2) existing loadable config: prefill every field via Value(&v) binding — validate stored enum values against the *Values() vocabularies before binding (huh Select silently lands on index 0 on mismatch); (3) existing broken config: print the loader's error (line info + vocabularies) and offer fresh-start overwrite or abort — no salvage decoder. Prompts derive from config.SchemaLangValues/QueryLangValues/DriverValues; path inputs and package name validate inline (non-empty; token.IsIdentifier). Config-file-only writes: soft-warn when schema/queries paths don't exist, never create them; print a next-steps epilogue. Final step: preview the exact canonical file Save will write and confirm; when the existing file contains comments, state they will not survive the rewrite. Old-version files migrate naturally (Load normalises, Save writes latest).

title: codegen: testcontainer-backed live smoke test for generated repositories
description:
Owner-flagged idea (2026-07-11): spin up a real neo4j v5-community via testcontainers-go and verify that the emitted repository code actually queries a live driver — not just type-checks against v5.28.4.

Scope suggestion (per C1 orchestrator brief):
- internal/codegen/live_test.go with build-tag codegen_live so CI stays opt-in.
- Testcontainer running neo4j:5-community.
- Steps:
  1. Generate a repo from a valid fixture (probably many_col_many for a decent surface).
  2. Compile the generated package into a small test binary.
  3. Seed the neo4j with a few known rows.
  4. Run the test binary against the live db.
  5. Assert :one and :many methods return the expected data (Row struct field values, ErrNoRows, ErrMultipleResults).

Rationale for deferring past C1:
- The goldens and just test-codegen-fence already prove the emitted code compiles + lints under the pinned driver — the type-check gap is closed.
- Adding testcontainers introduces a Docker dep and CI-time increase that changes the character of the internal/codegen test suite (pure Go today).
- The gap this closes is 'compiles + lints' vs 'actually queries' — real, but not blocking C1's DoD. Better to land it as its own PR cycle with the Docker-CI wiring done deliberately.

Priority: medium — worth doing before v1 GA so the emitted-code contract is battle-tested against a real driver.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #247 (feat(codegen): live testcontainer smoke test for generated repositories). Testcontainer-backed neo4j:5-community harness with codegen_live build tag; verifies emitted repo code queries a live driver. GH #227 auto-closed via 'Closes #227' in PR body. Agent completed while CI pending, so bead close deferred to orchestrator housekeeping post-merge.

title: Model unfreeze: CALL-arg attribution dropped on the wire — no argument-site type check possible
description: OWNER DECISION REQUIRED (ADR 0008 revision protocol; freeze-not-a-wall policy). The frozen wire model discards CALL argument attribution entirely: internal/query/cypher/call.go:54-66 mines each argument expression's type then DISCARDS it (explicit bucket-3 comment), emitting parameter uses as untagged ExprUse{t, ExprInProjection} and argument refs as bare names into curPart.refs. query.CallBinding (query.go:843-910) carries no arg-type/arg-ref/CallArg fields. The Stage-14 parser spec §4.5 (docs/specs/cypher-query-parser-stage-14.md:971-990) skiplists the arg-vs-param type check as bucket-3 by design. Consequence: ADR 0009's R7 line 'argument assignability including NUMBER assignable-from INTEGER-or-FLOAT (ADR 0007 Stage-14 note)' has NO application site in the resolver — R7 shipped YIELD typing + gate lift + parser-authoritative unknown-procedure only, with the delta documented in docs/specs/resolver-stage-r7.md §7.1.1. Proposed widening: per-position CALL-arg records on CallBinding (arg query.Type + param name/token from the matched procsig.Signature) emitted at parse; the resolver's argument-site check incl. NUMBER assignable-from INTEGER-or-FLOAT then becomes a subset/assignability walk over those records. Same attribution family as gqlc-fvo (Use→Part) — consider deciding both together.
close_reason: Delivered across PRs #121 (spec), #122 (model unfreeze: CallBinding.args axis, ADR 0008 amendment), #123 (resolver argument-site assignability walk incl. NUMBER assignable-from + TypeNull wildcard, sentinel ErrCallArgAssignability, 4 new fixtures), #125 (spec errata: fabricated witnesses corrected, census pinned, TypeNull E1), #124 (R7 §7.1.1 closed-out errata). Follow-up filed: gqlc-8en (R7 §1/§4.8 stale posture prose). Session note: cycle survived two teammate incidents (rogue merge, protection-deletion attack) — see bd memory unfreeze-loop-incident.

title: Model unfreeze: Use→Part attribution — parameter Uses carry no Part field
description: OWNER DECISION REQUIRED (ADR 0008 revision protocol; freeze-not-a-wall policy). The wire model carries no Use→Part attribution: PropertyUse/ExprUse/ClauseSlotUse in internal/query/query.go have no Part field (grep-verified during R5 code round 1). Consequence: the resolver cannot attribute a parameter PropertyUse to the Part it lexically occurs in, so R5 ships the any-valid-witness rule (resolve.go witnessAcrossScopes: witness in EVERY scope containing the Ref's variable, keep successes, unify via R2 lattice, error only when all containing scopes fail with ErrUnknownProperty). This is honest but has quantified residual unsoundness documented in docs/specs/resolver-stage-r5.md §4.2.4 (a Use valid only in a scope other than its lexical Part is admitted) and a §7 unfreeze row (commit 28cab79). Proposed: add Part index (or Part ref) to the Use records at emission. Would let the resolver witness exactly in the lexical scope and delete the any-valid-witness workaround.
close_reason: Cycle 2 complete: spec PR #116 (81321f0), unfreeze PR #117 (9d5b565, additive part int on 3 Use variants + ADR 0008 amendment, zero-golden rebaseline), resolver-widening PR #118 (d68fc75, Part-exact witness retires R5 §4.2.4 any-valid-witness), errata PR #119 (448eb47). Residuals filed: gqlc-4w5 (semantic-scope attribution), gqlc-qcc (Use.Branch axis).

title: Model unfreeze: ExprProjection.ContainsAggregate (Shape B) — nested aggregates invisible to resolver
description: OWNER DECISION REQUIRED (ADR 0008 revision protocol; freeze-not-a-wall policy). Frozen model drops nested-aggregate structure at classification: classifyRichExpression (internal/query/cypher/typing.go:857-877) unconditionally returns NewExprProjection(refs, t), so RETURN count(n)+1 is indistinguishable from RETURN n.x+1. R5 handled this via the uniform-exclude posture (ExprProjection never a grouping key => under-grouping when mixed with AggregateProjection; spec docs/specs/resolver-stage-r5.md §7.1.5 + audit note). Proposed Shape B: add a ContainsAggregate bool to ExprProjection, set by classifyRichExpression during the walk it already performs (parser-side, no resolver re-parse). Shapes A (OriginalText) and C (TextSpan) were evaluated and retired: recovered text re-parses opaque. Deferral lock to flip: parser pin 'count in arithmetic' (parser_test.go:1319-1327). Unblocks exact grouping-key semantics for mixed Parts.
close_reason: Delivered: spec PR #111 (17c4d18), unfreeze PR #112 (e0e2c83 — containsAggregate field + walker + ADR amendment + 20-golden rebaseline), resolver-widening PR #113 (0c0f076 — fillGroupingKeys gate+loop over ContainsAggregate, 2 fixtures, 116-golden byte-identity fence). title: Preserve AggregateFunc kind for aggregates over rich expressions
description: Reviewer SF-3 from Stage 10 (PR for gqlc-2av): when an aggregate's argument is not a bare var/literal/star (sum(x+1), count(n.age+1), collect(a OR b), count(count(*)), min([1,2,3]), sum(range(1,3))), classifyProjection falls through to ExprProjection and the AggregateFunc kind is silently dropped — resolver cannot distinguish an aggregate column from a plain expression of the same type. Inherited from Stage 6; Stage 10's DISTINCT rationale (two observably-different queries must not lower to indistinguishable models) applies verbatim. Fix direction: classifyFunction recognizes aggregate invocations with expression arguments and keeps the outer kind, or a dedicated axis on ExprProjection. Spec stage-10 §8 documents the gap.
close_reason: Landed in PR #79 (squash-merged; GH #78 auto-closed). classifyFunction gains aggregate-name-first dispatch into classifyAggregateCall: rich aggregate args (arithmetic, boolean composites, list literals, nested calls, parameters, count(*)) lower as AggregateProjection with kind/refs/DISTINCT preserved via typeExpressionMining; parameter Uses preserved verbatim per Stage 6 §4 (ExprUse{aggregateResultType(fn,operand), ExprInProjection} — count($p) keeps TypeInt). Nested aggregates (count(n)+1) stay ExprProjection as documented deferral; resolver contract recorded on gqlc-gyw (re-parse first, post-freeze additive ContainsAggregate axis as escape hatch). 75 goldens rebaselined (128 kind swaps, purity mechanically verified); suite 3897/3459/438/0 unchanged. Adversarial review: spec FAIL round 1 (4 blockers incl. sum($p) Use-regression catch), revision PASS round 2, full PASS round 3 with 4/4 mutants killed. title: Release automation: release-please (tag per release, no binaries)
description: Set up automated release tagging on master. Decision (2026-07-01): tag per RELEASE, not per merge — release-please-action maintains a rolling Release PR on pushes to master; merging it creates the semver tag + GitHub Release + CHANGELOG. Version bumps computed from Conventional Commit PR titles (squash-merge via Graphite means master commits = PR titles, so shift titles to feat:/fix:/etc). DESCOPED (2026-07-01): GoReleaser/binaries — main.go is a dev scratch harness with no real CLI surface, and user decided we likely never ship binaries; consumers use go install / go get. Releases = tag + version + GitHub Release with changelog (layers 1-3). If a real CLI ever emerges and binaries are wanted, file a new bead.
design: 1) .github/workflows/release-please.yml on push to master, token fallback: secrets.RELEASE_PLEASE_TOKEN || GITHUB_TOKEN (PAT needed once required status checks exist, else Release PR gets no CI runs). 2) release-please-config.json: release-type=go, bump-minor-pre-major=true (feat!: bumps minor while 0.x). 3) .release-please-manifest.json seeded at 0.0.0 — first Release PR should propose 0.1.0; if bootstrap proposes something odd, force with release-as then remove. 4) .github/CODEOWNERS per notes. 5) Tag ruleset (pattern v*) in GitHub UI restricting creation. 6) No just recipe: release-please is GitHub-side API automation, not a check — nothing to run locally, so CI-parity rule doesn't apply.
acceptance_criteria: Merging PRs to master updates a rolling Release PR; merging the Release PR creates v* tag + GitHub Release with changelog; no binary artifacts; manual tag pushes blocked by tag ruleset; CODEOWNERS present.
notes:
CODEOWNERS (do alongside this work, prep for future maintainers): add .github/CODEOWNERS with a catch-all line plus explicit entries pinning release/CI pipeline paths to the owner — these paths can mint releases / run arbitrary CI code, so they must keep owner review even after source dirs are delegated. Example (last matching pattern wins):
*                       @antranig-yeretzian
/.github/               @antranig-yeretzian
/.goreleaser.yaml       @antranig-yeretzian
/release-please-config.json @antranig-yeretzian
While solo the catch-all makes the rest redundant; value is documenting intent for when e.g. /internal/query/ gets a maintainer. Interaction: release-please's Release PR touches CHANGELOG.md + manifest, so once 'Require review from Code Owners' is enabled (maintainer-#2 day), the owner must approve every Release PR — acceptable/desirable: releases become an explicit owner decision. Patterns for not-yet-existing files are harmless (no-ops).

title: Resolver: undirected edge two-orientation trial + multi-match semantics
description: Post-freeze resolver contract handed off by Stage 5 (gqlc-7z6). An EdgeBinding with directed=false has NO authoritative orientation: the parser records endpoints in textual order only. The resolver MUST form TWO candidate schema.EdgeKeys (source->target AND target->source) and accept the binding if EITHER resolves against the directed-only schema. OPEN QUESTION (spec cypher-query-parser-stage-5.md §9): the trial can match zero, one, or TWO distinct directed edge types (schema has both A->B and B->A with the queried label) — define whether a double match is ambiguous, a union, or an error, and whether the resolver must report WHICH orientation(s) matched (which may require widening the marker bool->enum, cheap pre-consumer). Lives downstream of resolver bead gqlc-0mx and the freeze gqlc-cta.
notes: Linked to resolver stage R3 (gqlc-0mx.5) at the freeze (2026-07-03): the undirected two-orientation trial + double-match decision is R3's core design question; this bead carries the accumulated context.
close_reason: Decided and implemented in R3 (spec PR #95 §3.5/§4.6, code PR #96): resolver forms both orientation candidates; single-type single-hop undirected double-match => ErrAmbiguousEdgeOrientation (author disambiguates with directed arrow — no | union opt-in was written); multi-type >=2 matches => ResolvedEdgeUnion with canonically ordered EdgeKeys; matched orientation(s) inspectable from EdgeKey.Source/Target — no parser marker widening needed, query.Query freeze respected.

notes: Stage 3 on branch stage-3-projections (single branch, separated commits). DONE: (1) grill-with-docs -> spec docs/specs/cypher-query-parser-stage-3.md committed (d652c11). Restructured query.Query into Branches(UNION)->Parts(WITH) with UnionKind enum; per-part scope threading (exported names L-to-R, WITH * transitive, cross-branch isolation); parameters stay query-wide. godog read-core 371 passed / 179 pending (was 336/173 at Stage 3 end; +35 scenarios parse, clauses/with+clauses/union added to readCoreDirs). 7 WITH/UNION negatives skiplisted (all verified below the type-interface boundary by adversarial review). title: codegen-live: promote nightly to PR-blocking merge gate
description:
Promote .github/workflows/codegen-live.yml from workflow_dispatch +
nightly cron to a PR-blocking check on the merge gate. The codegen ->
driver contract is currently only exercised against a real neo4j once
a day; a regression that ships in a codegen refactor sits on master
for up to 24h before nightly surfaces it. Blocking on PR closes that
window.

Precondition: gqlc-9u5 (Go test cache enabled) must be merged first.
Without cache, this promotion imposes ~30s on every PR — violates the
ci-speed-parity invariant. With cache, a typical PR pays <1s
(cached PASS); only PRs that touch codegen-relevant paths pay ~30s.

Changes:
1. Add on.pull_request to codegen-live.yml (already has push:master +
   nightly cron; add pull_request).
2. Update the workflow docstring to reflect its new role — no longer
   an opt-in satellite, now a merge-gate.
3. Consider a paths: filter (belt + suspenders alongside Go's cache).
   Path candidates: internal/codegen/**, internal/resolver/**,
   internal/schema/**, internal/queryfile/**, test/data/codegen/**,
   .github/workflows/codegen-live.yml, justfile. Miss one and we'd
   falsely skip; skip if the cache is trusted enough.

Depends on: gqlc-9u5.
Descends from: gqlc-5gc scope discussion.

title: codegen-live: enable Go test cache in CI
description:
Wire the codegen-live workflow so Go's test cache actually works across
CI runs. Today \`just test-codegen-live\` (justfile:98) passes \`-count=1\`,
the canonical opt-out from Go's test result cache. Combined with
actions/setup-go@v5 defaulting cache-dependency-path to root go.sum
(missing the nested test/data/codegen/go.sum), we get zero cache hits
on the live-smoke workflow even when nothing that could affect the
generated code has changed.

Changes:
1. Remove \`-count=1\` from the test-codegen-live recipe. Go's cache
   invalidates automatically when any input file (source, test binary,
   env vars the test reads via os.Getenv) changes.
2. Add cache-dependency-path: |\n     go.sum\n     test/data/codegen/go.sum
   to actions/setup-go@v5 in .github/workflows/codegen-live.yml so
   nested-module dep bumps invalidate the cache correctly.
3. Pin neo4jImage in test/data/codegen/live_test.go from
   "neo4j:5-community" to a digest ("neo4j@sha256:..."). Mutable tags
   would allow neo4j to push a rebuild under the same string, and Go's
   cache — which trusts the string constant as the input — would serve
   a stale PASS. Digests are immutable, so any digest bump naturally
   invalidates the cache.

Result: cache-miss = full ~30s run (v5 + v6 arms in parallel).
Cache-hit = <1s "(cached)" without touching docker. Sets up the
follow-up bead promoting the workflow to PR-blocking.

Precedes: codegen-live PR-blocking promotion bead.
Descends from: gqlc-5gc scope discussion.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #322. Removed -count=1 from just test-codegen-live, added cache-dependency-path with both root + nested go.sum, pinned neo4jImage to multi-arch manifest-list digest sha256:362542416.... Cache now serves (cached) PASS on unchanged inputs; sets up gqlc-rvw's PR-blocking promotion.

title: codegen: investigate SingleT/CollectT shape for :one and :many emission
description:
Explore whether the generated :one and :many methods should route through
neo4j.SingleT[T]/CollectT[T] instead of the current \`records, err := q.db.run(...)\`
+ manual \`len(records)\` sentinel-branching shape (see generated
test/data/codegen/valid/*/golden/queries.cypher.go and db.go).

Trade-offs to evaluate:
  * Less emitted boilerplate per :one method
  * SingleT folds "zero rows" and "multiple rows" into one error — losing
    the current ErrNoRows / ErrMultipleResults sentinel granularity that
    is the semantic contract of :one. Any shape change must preserve
    (or deliberately drop, with owner sign-off) that contract.
  * :many currently materialises \`[]*neo4j.Record\` then decodes; a
    CollectT[T]-based path could inline the mapper.
  * Interacts with gqlc-1a5 (:iter streaming cardinality, post-v1).

Deferred from the gqlc-5gc scope discussion: verified v5 and v6 both
expose SingleT/CollectT, so this is a codegen-quality question, not a
driver-version question. Do the investigation before committing to a
shape.

title: cypher parser tests: rebaseline cost of the ~112 surviving authored mustParse pins (post-ls8.5 residual)
description: gqlc-ls8.5's mechanical census showed only 43 of 159 hand-built mustParse pins are shape-mirrors of existing goldens; ~112 are authored inputs the mustParse doctrine (parser_test.go preamble) and TestGoldenOrphans rightly forbid converting to goldens (evidentiary circularity: round-tripping launders current parser behavior into 'expected', and -update silently rebases thereafter). So the original 'maintenance outlier' complaint — every additive model axis costs manual edits across parser_test.go — is only PARTIALLY closed by ls8.5; the authored pins retain that cost. This bead evaluates what, if anything, reduces it without weakening evidence: candidates include (a) contributing authored scenarios upstream to the TCK corpus so they earn goldens legitimately, (b) helper/table consolidation so additive zero-valued axes are absorbed without per-case edits, (c) a narrowly-safeguarded doctrine amendment (would need a first-golden review protocol that answers the silent -update objection concretely). Deliberately open-ended; a 'no action, cost is the price of evidence' close is acceptable if analysis supports it.

comments.author: antranig-yeretzian
comments.text:
gqlc-ls8.5 §9 Q3 by-stage consolidation detail (queued at Phase-3 close;
released post-verdict per team-lead ratification of PR #308 at f33296a).

Recording the concrete shape of candidate (b) — helper/table consolidation —
as it surfaced during the ls8.5 Phase-1/2/3 grill so the option is
actionable when this bead moves. Not a proposal; a factual snapshot of the
residual pin population by stage, which the ls8.5 census incidentally
produced.

By-stage rebaseline surface (from the residual 117 mustParse entries at
Phase-3 tip 3,528, per §2 outturn table):

- Stage 4 (UNION-nesting): every mustParse pin that exercises multi-branch
  structure carries the same one-branch-per-part scaffolding through
  `oneBranch` / `oneWriteBranch`. An additive Stage-4 axis change (e.g. a
  new BranchState carrier field) forces per-pin manual edits across every
  authored UNION-nested pin. Contiguous rebaseline section: group these
  pins into a Stage-4 subsection of the mustParse map so an axis-change
  touches N helper-lines instead of N-pins.

- Stage 12 (write-side statements): every pin using `oneWriteBranch`
  asserts on the StatementKind axis end-to-end. An additive write-side
  axis (statement-scope, per-clause write metadata, etc.) forces the same
  pattern of per-pin manual edits. Contiguous rebaseline section: group
  Stage-12 write pins together so the helper carries the axis burden.

- Stage 14 (CALL / procsig-carrying pins): the residual sig-divergent
  keeper + any future Class-A sig-carrying pins whose sigs diverge from
  Background sigs. An additive procsig axis (new `procsig.Signature`
  field, per-CallBinding metadata) forces manual edits across every
  Stage-14 pin. Contiguous rebaseline section: group Stage-14 CALL pins
  together with a shared authored-sigs helper so the axis-change
  touches the helper, not per-pin bodies.

Rationale: the ~20.7 gross lines/pin cost observed in the ls8.5 Phase-2
sweep (871 lines across 42 deletions; see spec §2 actuals table) is
dominated by the helper-invocation lines, not the pin body itself. A
consolidation that lifts the axis-touching lines into shared helpers
converts per-pin manual edits into per-helper manual edits without
weakening the independent-evidence property (the hand-derived Query
shape stays hand-derived; only the constructor scaffolding is shared).

Constraint the consolidation must preserve: the reviewer diffing an
axis-rebaseline PR must still be able to read each pin's expected
shape independently — the helper cannot become so terse that the pin's
target shape is inferred rather than stated. A helper that returns a
pre-shaped Query is a step too far; a helper that takes the axis values
as parameters and returns the composed Query is the ceiling.

Cross-refs: cypher-golden-test-migration.md §6 (per-pin cost analysis),
§9 Q3 (the parent question this comment operationalises). PR #308 sha
f33296a shipped the ls8.5 residual figures this consolidation would
target.

comments.author: antranig-yeretzian
comments.text:
Analysis (autonomous, 2026-07-18):

Verified census. `parser_test.go` at HEAD is 3,528 lines; `mustParse` runs L125-2610 and holds **117 pins** (matches ls8.5 Q1 outturn: 43+4+112 pre-migration, then 42 Class-A shape-mirrors deleted; residual = 117). Helper concentration inside the map:

- `oneBranch(query.Part{...})` — 74 top-level uses (60 pre-Stage-12, 1 Stage-13 "MERGE inside EXISTS does not flip", 14 Stage-14).
- `oneWriteBranch(query.Part{...})` — 12 top-level uses (9 Stage-12 write pins, 3 Stage-13 MERGE-write pins).
- 30 inline `query.Query{Branches: []query.Branch{{Parts: []query.Part{...}}}}` builds + 1 `func() query.Query {...}()` composer, all pre-Stage-12. Of the 30, 3 are true UNION shape (multi-branch: "union two branches", "union param branch attribution", "union all two branches"), ~7 are true WITH-chain shape (multi-part in one branch: "optional group ids span parts without reset", "with chain whole entity", "with chain value projection", "unwind list of lists", "return order by param", "with distinct", "exists body param via with-where", "unwind of list-of-nodes reused as node match"), and ~20 are single-branch/single-Part pins written raw — mostly Stage-6/7 temporal-arithmetic pins ("return date constructor", "return duration between", the six duration/date arithmetic pins around L637-682) plus a few Parameter-carrying pins that predate `oneBranch`'s variadic Parameters signature. These 20 could migrate to `oneBranch` today with no design change.

Stage bucketing of the 117 by file-position: Stages 0-11 hold 90 pins (concentrated in Stage 6 rich-projection/temporal, Stage 8 pattern shapes, and Stage 11 EXISTS-body pins — 15+ EXISTS-body pins in the L1397-1938 range dominate this segment); Stage 12 write pins hold 9; Stage 13 MERGE holds 4; Stage 14 CALL holds 14. Sig-carrying pins total 12 (11 with populated `sigs`, the sole `sigDivergentKeepers` member the survival case). The 3-way clustering the beads comment names (Stage 4 UNION-nesting, Stage 12 write-side, Stage 14 CALL/procsig) is only partly correct: Stage-14 CALL is a real 14-pin cluster with uniform `oneBranch`+`sigs` scaffolding; Stage-12 write is a real 9-pin cluster on `oneWriteBranch`; but Stage-4 UNION-nesting is only 3 pins — the comment's framing of "every authored UNION-nested pin" over-counts by conflating "multi-Part WITH chains" (~7 pins) with UNION (3 pins).

Empirical axis-cost check against the shipped model-change specs, which is the load-bearing calibration the bead is missing: **ay9** flipped **3 mustParse pins** (spec §4.3, L84-91: "optional match simple", "optional match reuses prior binding", "count distinct" — 4 `NewNullable*` invocations become `*InGroup(..., 1)`); the other 149 Binding-constructor invocations in the file stayed verbatim under `require.Equal`. **hk0** flipped **1 pin** ("count in arithmetic"); the other 24 ExprProjection assertions stayed verbatim (Shape B's zero-valued `ContainsAggregate` is bit-identical). **0ig** touched **11 pins** carrying 15 CallBinding invocations, of which only those with ≥1 argument rebaseline (spec §4.3). **5xg** flipped **0 pins** — Go zero-value equality holds. Ledger: 3 / 1 / 11 / 0. The bead description's "every additive model axis costs manual edits across ~112 pins" is empirically wrong; the actual cost per shipped axis has been 0-11 pins, and each cluster was the exact subset whose src exercised the changed axis.

Candidate evaluation. **(a) Upstream to TCK.** The 112 Class-C pins are authored precisely because the TCK is silent on the sentinel taxonomy they encode: 5xg's required-bare-ref flag, fvo's Use-part attribution, ay9's per-clause OptionalGroup — none of these are surface Cypher semantics the openCypher project would accept as scenarios. A handful (some MERGE and CALL kill-probes) plausibly map to legitimate skeptical scenarios, but even a maximally-successful contribution cycle would land ~5-10 scenarios upstream over several TCK bumps, converting <10% of the Class-C set at best, on a multi-quarter cadence outside gqlc's control. Cost/removal ratio is poor.

**(b) Helper/table consolidation.** The relevant axis cost per shipped spec is 0-11 pins per axis, i.e. it is not concentrated across all Class-C pins — the axis touches only pins whose src exercises it. Under `require.Equal` a helper that hides an axis inside its scaffolding gives you nothing you don't already have from Go zero-value equality: hk0's 24 non-flipping ExprProjection pins pay ZERO edit cost today. What consolidation could buy: for the 14 Stage-14 CALL pins, a helper `oneCallBranch(part query.Part, sigs []procsig.Signature, params ...query.Parameter) query.Query` co-locates the `sigs` field with the branch so any procsig axis change edits the helper; for the 3 UNION pins, `twoBranchUnion(a query.Branch, b query.Branch, kind query.UnionKind, params ...query.Parameter)` closes the raw-Branches inlining. Both preserve the "reviewer reads each pin's expected shape independently" constraint IF the helper returns a composed `query.Query` from parameterized inputs, not a pre-shaped Query. The real gain is measured in per-cluster line counts, not per-axis edits — which is a code-hygiene win, not a maintenance-outlier fix.

**(c) Doctrine amendment.** The `-update` laundering objection is concrete: minting a golden from an authored src runs the parser once under `-update`, records that output as expected, and every subsequent `-update` silently rebaselines through the same parser. A first-golden review protocol would need to (1) prohibit `-update` from ever touching an authored-origin golden except through a two-reviewer human sign-off that inspects the diff against the model contract, and (2) tag each authored golden at mint time with the ADR/spec §-ref that ratified its shape. Both are hard to enforce in code — `-update` is one flag on `go test`, not a per-file gate. Any protocol short of a file-level `//go:build authored-golden` guard that fails `-update` unless overridden by an environment variable will drift under time pressure. The safeguard exists as an idea but doesn't answer the objection concretely — it just adds a review layer over the same silent-rebase mechanism.

Recommendation. **Adopt a narrow (b) targeted at Stage-14, with 20-pin `oneBranch` migration cleanup as a separable follow-on.** Not (a) — the upstream conversion rate is too low. Not (c) — the doctrine amendment does not answer the `-update` objection concretely enough to override the lead ruling that stands from ls8.5 §1.4/§8. Not "no action" — the Stage-14 CALL cluster is a genuine 14-pin uniform-scaffold surface where an axis addition would touch every pin; consolidation here is a real win, not a code-hygiene wash.

Concrete helper signatures for (b):

```go
// oneCallBranch composes a Stage-14 one-branch/one-part CALL pin: same
// shape as oneBranch, plus authored procedure sigs the parser needs for
// registry-backed CALL resolution. Every Stage-14 mustParse pin uses
// this so a procsig axis change touches the helper, not per-pin bodies.
func oneCallBranch(
    part query.Part,
    sigs []procsig.Signature,
    params ...query.Parameter,
) (query.Query, []procsig.Signature)
```

Returning both the Query and the `sigs` slice lets the pin body destructure into `want, sigs` at the call site — the pin still names its `sigs` in-place, but any future field addition on `procsig.Signature` (a per-CallBinding metadata axis, an authored-vs-Background provenance bit, etc.) edits `oneCallBranch` once. Applies to all 14 Stage-14 pins.

```go
// twoBranchUnion composes a Stage-4 two-branch UNION pin: two branches
// (each supplied as an already-built Branch), a single combinator, and
// query-level Parameters. Every UNION pin in mustParse uses this so a
// combinator or per-branch metadata axis touches the helper, not the pin.
func twoBranchUnion(
    a query.Branch,
    b query.Branch,
    kind query.UnionKind,
    params ...query.Parameter,
) query.Query
```

Applies to 3 pins ("union two branches", "union param branch attribution", "union all two branches").

The 20 raw-inlined single-branch/single-Part pins ("return date constructor" through "return duration between", plus the temporal/arithmetic follow-ons at L637-682, plus the parameter-carrying pins that predate `oneBranch`'s variadic signature) should be migrated to `oneBranch(query.Part{...}, params...)` in a separable stylistic PR — no design change, no axis cost, just eliminating one class of divergent scaffolding a future reviewer would trip over. Explicitly NOT part of this bead's decision; log it as a separate low-priority code-hygiene bead. Not scoping to Stage 12 either: `oneWriteBranch` already exists and every Stage-12 write pin uses it. Not adding a "WithChain" helper — the 7 multi-Part WITH-chain pins each have a unique Part composition (part 1's Returns are the WITH projection; part 2's Bindings are what the MATCH-after-WITH introduces; the shapes diverge structurally); a helper here would either return a pre-shaped Query (violates the reviewer-reads-pin-shape constraint) or take so many parameters it's less legible than the inline form.

Constraints for future work. A bad first attempt at (b) would introduce a helper that materializes the pin's target shape from an axis argument the pin never names — e.g. `stage14CallPin(name string) query.Query` that hard-codes each pin's expected Query by key. That launders the same evidentiary circularity the mustParse doctrine forbids (the "expected" shape is now hidden in Go code the reviewer must dereference to read). The doctrine cliff: any helper whose return value contains a Bindings/Returns/Effects slice the caller did not pass in is over the line — the helper must be a pure composer of caller-supplied parts. A second cliff to avoid: coupling `oneCallBranch` to `sigs` provenance (Background-equal vs authored-divergent) — the coupling belongs at pin declaration site (as the current `sigDivergentKeepers` allowlist encodes it), not inside the helper. Signal of a bad attempt: the helper signature grows past ~4 parameters, or the pin body loses the ability to be read as "this src produces this shape" without stepping into the helper.

The bead's original headline — "every additive axis costs manual edits across ~112 authored pins" — should be closed as OVERSTATED at ls8.5 close. The load-bearing 112 Class-C pins are cheap under `require.Equal` for every axis whose zero value coincides with the pre-axis shape; the actual per-axis cost has been 0-11 pins, and each of those was the exact subset whose src exercised the axis, which is the cost of evidence, not of scaffolding. (b) removes a real 14-pin surface at Stage 14 without weakening evidence; the residual across Stages 0-11 stays authored, per doctrine, and per the empirical 0-3 pin-touch cost the shipped model-change record proves.

Independent verification: 117-pin count reproduced (main-checkout grep against L125-2610); 74/12/30/1 helper decomposition inspected; agent-attributed axis-flip counts (ay9=3, hk0=1, 0ig=11, 5xg=0) NOT independently re-verified against each PR's diff — best-effort agent claim. Owner call: pick between narrow (b) at Stage-14, "no action pending a real axis demand", or defer to a specific follow-up bead for the 14-pin oneCallBranch cutover.

title: codegen: v6 arm for the live-smoke testcontainer (TestLiveSmoke)
description: just test-codegen-live / TestLiveSmoke proves generated repositories against a real neo4j testcontainer, but only for the v5 driver: it hard-imports two v5 golden fixture packages and constructs a v5 driver. title: codegen: v6-only column types — Vector + UUID (schema→resolver→codegen)
description: neo4j-go-driver v6 adds dbtype.Vector (Bolt 6.0) and native UUID (Bolt 6.1, v6.2.0). Mapping these as gqlc column types requires the full pipeline: schema property types, graph.PropertyType, resolver ResolvedType, codegen type tables (gated on DriverV6 — reject or fall back under DriverV5). Deliberately out of scope for the v6-option stack: no vector/uuid anywhere in internal/graph or internal/schema today, so this is schema-layer design work first. Also evaluated and deferred: v6.2 object-mapping preview API (unstable, preview-labelled — do not codegen against it); session.Run one-shot retry ADR-038 (generated code uses ExecuteRead/ExecuteWrite closures, no win).
design:
Design research banked 2026-07-12 (verified against driver 6.x branch + Neo4j docs):

DRIVER SHAPES: dbtype.UUID = [16]byte (RFC 9562, ParseUUID/String/TextMarshaler). dbtype.Vector[T VectorElement], VectorElement = float64|float32|int8|int16|int32|int64. neo4j.PropertyValue constraint (graph.go:25-30) already includes UUID and ALL SIX Vector instantiations -> generated decode via neo4j.GetProperty/GetRecordValue works unchanged; no new decode machinery.

SCHEMA SPELLING: adopt Cypher 25 official syntax VECTOR<FLOAT32>(1024) (element + dimension; used in IS :: constraints, Neo4j 2025.10+, requires v6 drivers). UUID: no GQL-standard spelling exists; propose bare UUID keyword.

GRAMMAR DELTA (the hard part): GQL.g4 valueType/predefinedType has NEITHER — UUID needs a predefinedType alternative + keyword token; VECTOR needs a parameterized rule (angle-bracket element + parenthesized dimension). Regen = just build-grammar = docker + ANTLR 4.13.1.

PIPELINE MAP: spellings table internal/schema/gql/propertytype.go:53-108 (canonicalSpelling normalization); enum internal/graph/propertytype.go:9-39 — NB flat enum cannot carry Vector's element+dimension: main modeling decision is PropertyType growing structure (or parallel fields) — deserves grilling/ADR. Resolver: ResolvedProperty passes graph.PropertyType through (resolve.go:1300,1345) — no resolver enum change for property-sourced columns. Codegen v5-rejection precedent: goType ok=false -> ErrUnrepresentableWidth at prepare.go:410/498/560 + types.go:254; UUID/Vector under DriverV5 should mirror it with a new error (requires-driver-v6), which means threading driverTarget into the prepare phases (today target only reaches render).

STAGING: (1) UUID first — no parameterization: keyword + TypeUUID + goType v6 arm + v5 rejection + _v6 goldens. (2) Vector second — grammar param type + PropertyType shape change, grill the modeling with the user first. Dimension: store for future constraint emission; Go carrier ignores it (Vector.Elems is a plain slice).
notes: 2026-07-12: claim released — implementation blocked on this host: grammar regen needs docker (none) or java (none). Design fully banked in DESIGN field; UUID stage is mechanical once grammar tooling is available; Vector stage needs a PropertyType-shape grill with the user first.

title: resolver: builtin scalar-function signature table (elementId → string)
description: ADR 0010 D3 resolved entity structs as schema-shaped only; driver identity is accessed via explicit projection: RETURN p, elementId(p) AS id. The resolver currently types function invocations as unknown, so the alias renders as 'any' in generated code. Add a small builtin scalar-function signature table to the resolver — elementId(node|edge) -> string is the motivating entry; the same mechanism serves other builtins (id(), count(), etc.) later. When it lands, the recommended identity pattern types as string with zero codegen changes. Not blocking any C-stage.

Parser-side builtin scalar-function signature table wired into classifyFunction + typeAtom; motivating entry elementId(node|edge) -> string plus id(node|edge) -> int. Codegen fixture identity_element_id/ pins the ADR 0010 D3 identity pattern rendering Id string (was any).

title: Codegen: :iter streaming cardinality (ADR 0010 D8, post-v1)
description: Fourth cardinality :iter generating streaming methods — func (q *Queries) X(ctx, ...) iter.Seq2[Row, error] — per ADR 0010 D8. Rationale: avoid sqlc's structural weakness where []Row materialisation makes full-result loads easy and streaming unretrofittable; neo4j driver streams natively and range-over-func scopes session cleanup to loop exit (early break included). v1 reserves the space only: cardinality is an open enum, method shapes assume no materialisation. Open design questions to settle when picked up: author-declared :iter vs auto-generated XIter sibling for every :many; interplay with SKIP/LIMIT pagination params; requires Go >= 1.23 in the user's module.
notes:
Design opens transferred from ADR 0010 D8 (grill close-out, 2026-07-11): (1) :iter as author-declared cardinality vs an auto-generated XIter sibling for every :many; (2) interaction with SKIP/LIMIT pagination parameters (orthogonal — document both). v1 committed exactly two enablers: cardinality is an open enum (annotation grammar admits :iter without a break) and nothing in the D2 method shape assumes materialisation. Session-lifetime hazard owns the design: iter.Seq2 scopes cleanup to loop exit incl. early break.

Design opens resolved (grill, 2026-07-11): ADR 0010 §D8 amended in PR #250 with seven Resolved-grill markers. Summary:
- Author-declared :iter opt-in (auto-XIter siblings rejected)
- :iter read-only; ErrIterOnWrite sentinel rejects writes
- Cardinality×shape validation extends symmetrically
- Method naming inherits §D2 verbatim
- Retry envelope: pre-first-yield only (post-yield errors yielded to caller, no double-yield)
- SKIP/LIMIT orthogonal
- Testing: goldens + gqlc-73h live smoke extension
Universal :iter (kill :many) considered and tabled for later revisit.
Implementation trigger: gated on user demand or benchmarked need. Grill markers in ADR are the paved runway.

title: GH↔bd sync: bd→GH close/update propagation for already-mirrored beads
description:
The GH↔bd sync hooks (PR #201) cover three directions:
- Post-merge pull: GH → bd (creates, closes, comments) — works.
- Session-start pull: GH → bd — works.
- Pre-push push: bd → GH — but ONLY for beads without external_ref (i.e., NEW beads).

Uncovered: bd → GH status/comment updates on ALREADY-MIRRORED beads. When an agent runs 'bd close gqlc-abc' on a bead that already has a GH mirror, the pre-push hook's filter (external_ref == null) skips it, and the GH mirror stays open until the human closes it manually.

Root cause: the fast filter needed to avoid the ~30s cost of 'bd github sync --push-only' (which scans every bead against GH) doesn't cover updates on existing beads. Session memory kv.memory.bd-github-sync-quirks notes that even --push-only may not reliably propagate close status (bd bug).

Options to close the gap (design decision pending):
1. Accept it — treat GH as the canonical close-decision surface; agent closes on bd are recommendations, human closes on GH are authoritative. Requires human touch on every close.
2. Track dirty beads via a marker file that bd updates on close/update, and pre-push consumes and clears.
3. Wait for a bd feature that emits close-since-last-sync events (upstream bd change).
4. Fall back to 'bd github sync --push-only' (30s) only when a fast preflight detects a locally-closed bead that has an external_ref. Requires a per-bead updated_at vs GH-updated_at compare that bd currently only exposes via full sync.

No consumer is blocked; the workflow degrades gracefully (human sees stale-open GH issue, closes manually, next post-merge propagates the close back into bd).
close_reason: Shipped in PR #318 (squash 81e5bec). Extended .githooks/bd-gh-sync push path: bulk gh issue list --state open intersected with closed-bd beads bearing external_ref; each mismatch gets gh issue close with a comment derived from close_reason. Verified live on push: 13 stale mismatches accumulated since PR #201 all closed cleanly (GH open 26→13, matching real open-bead count). Silent, idempotent, non-fatal. All CI green (tidy/lint/test/actionlint/codegen-fence/govulncheck).

title: Model / resolver: edge-side non-bare OPTIONAL-edge re-reference witness (post-5xg residual)
description: Post-5xg residual, filed at unfreeze cycle 5 close-out. The 5xg widening (PR #134) closes same-Part Class B same-Part bare-pattern demotion for both NodeBinding and EdgeBinding. However, the parser's bare-predicate (bare == chain-head with empty chain) is grammatically unreachable for edge variables — every edge sits between two nodes in a chain link. Consequently, an OPTIONAL-introduced edge re-referenced by name in a required chain link (e.g. OPTIONAL MATCH (a)-[r:R]->(b) MATCH (c)-[r]->(d) RETURN r) has ReferencedInRequiredBarePattern()==false and stays nullable under both 5xg and ay9 (its group has no proven member; itself is the OPTIONAL binding, not a witness). Neither Class A nor Class B closes this case. Candidate closures: (1) widen the 5xg parser predicate to admit edges in required chain links (would set flag on r); (2) add a distinct axis (e.g. ReferencedInRequiredChain []ClauseRef or bool); (3) treat this as an ay9 refinement (a same-name required chain-edge witnesses the OPTIONAL edge's group). Design decision pending. This is a legitimate under-demotion (lattice-safe under R4 §4.2) with a small blast radius (only OPTIONAL edges re-referenced by name in required chains).

title: Resolver: cross-Part OPTIONAL-group carry via branchState (post-ay9 residual)
description: Post-ay9 residual, filed at unfreeze cycle 4 close-out. The ay9 widening (PR #129) closes same-Part Class A OPTIONAL-clause-sibling demotion via a group-closure fixed point in demoteNullableInPlace. Cross-Part carry across WITH is unchanged: the resolver-internal branchState.exportedNullableBinding (internal/resolver/resolve.go:357-361) is name to bool and does not carry OPTIONAL-group ids. A binding carried across WITH keeps its own Nullable bit but loses its ay9-widened group closure — the downstream Part's demoter cannot see the source Part's OPTIONAL-group membership, so a WITH-carried OPTIONAL binding subsequently proven demotes only itself, not its group-siblings from the source Part. Extend branchState to carry OPTIONAL-group ids alongside the nullable bit (resolver-internal — no model change; ADR 0008 unaffected). Detail in docs/specs/unfreeze-ay9-optional-group.md sections 2.5 and 7 (residual note). This is a normal resolver refinement, not a model unfreeze.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #253 (feat(resolver): cross-Part OPTIONAL-group carry via branchState). Extended branchState.exportedOptionalGroup (name → int) so WITH-carried OPTIONAL bindings preserve group-id membership across Part boundaries. Group closure in demoteNullableInPlace unions carried and local memberships. Resolver-internal, no model change, ADR 0008 unaffected. New fixture demote_cross_with_optional_group.cypher; existing golden demote_bare_reference_composes_with_group flipped from Class A under-demote to tight demote (bonus 5xg §8.4 fixture 4 boundary lift). Spec ay9 §2.5+§7 amended. Recovered from stalled agent by orchestrator; force-push --lease after gci fix.

title: Use.Branch axis: UNION cross-branch attribution residuals (post-fvo)
description: Follow-up from unfreeze cycle 2 (gqlc-fvo, PR #118). Use records carry Part but no BRANCH index; the frozen model cannot derive per-branch Use counts (WHERE-body Uses leave no footprint on Part.Bindings/Returns/Effects — fvo spec erratum 2, §7.2.2 countUsesInBranch unimplementable). Shipped recovery: position cursor with Part-index-drop boundary detection + witnessInBranch cross-branch fallback at the SAME Part index on ErrUnknownProperty. Two verified residuals (linus, PR #118 review; no corpus fixture triggers either): (a) same-Part UNION boundary ambiguity — a Use invalid in its true branch but valid in a sibling branch at the same Part is admitted via fallback (any-valid-witness survives in the cross-branch direction only); (b) UNION-later-Part spurious rejection — branch 0 with fewer Parts than the Use's Part index hits ErrOutOfR0Scope on the primary try and the fallback does NOT recover from non-ErrUnknownProperty errors, so a Use genuinely in branch 1 Part 1 is spuriously rejected. Fix: additive 'branch int' axis on Use records under ADR 0008 revision protocol (or equivalent attribution), then delete the cursor+fallback recovery. See fvo spec §7.2.2 erratum and R5 §4.2.4 cross-refs.
notes: Linus PASS (2026-07-12, PR #262). Two non-blocking NITs recorded for posterity: (1) resolve.go ~407-409 retains stale pre-fvo comment prose describing the old cursor recovery narrative — candidate for a drive-by prose fix next time that region is touched; (2) ADR 0008 amendment claim 'no golden carries a Use outside branch 0' is vacuously true (no golden serializes Use records at all) — wording could be tightened. Neither affects correctness.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #262 (squash-merged 2026-07-12). Added Branch attribution axis to all three Use variants, stamped once at the addParameterUse emission chokepoint; resolver routes Uses directly via tables[u.Branch()] (range-checked to ErrOutOfR0Scope); deleted witnessInBranch/usePart/cursor+Part-drop recovery entirely. Both verified residuals pinned: unknown_property_union_sibling_branch.cypher (residual a, now invalid) and parameter_union_later_part.cypher (residual b, now valid, golden STRING NOT NULL). Spec closure in fvo §7.2.2/§7.2.1/§12, R5 §4.2.4, ADR 0008 dated amendment. Linus PASS; two non-blocking NITs in notes.

title: Semantic-scope attribution: WITH…WHERE aliased-shadow residual (post-fvo)
description: Follow-up from unfreeze cycle 2 (gqlc-fvo, PRs #116-#118). Part attribution on Use records is EMISSION-TIME, which diverges from Cypher's post-projection semantics in two documented shapes: (1) FINDING-9 (fvo spec §7.6.1): WITH-trailing WHERE PropertyUses mine PRE-swap (mineWhere at listener.go:283-284 runs before the Part swap at :292-293), so 'MATCH (a:Post) WITH a.title AS a WHERE a.x = $p RETURN a' attributes Ref{a,x} to Part 0 (a=Post) and the resolver ADMITS if Post has x, though Cypher evaluates the WHERE post-projection (a=STRING). No regression vs pre-fvo any-valid-witness, but not full Cypher semantics. (2) Intra-clause asymmetry (found in PR #117 review): a direct param in 'WITH n WHERE $x' lands Part 0 (pre-swap mineWhere) while an EXISTS-body param in the same syntactic WHERE lands Part 1 (natural walk post-swap, listener.go:627-638; parser pin 1712). Closing this needs a SEMANTIC-scope attribution axis (which scope evaluates the predicate), not the lexical Part axis — a distinct model decision, likely another ADR 0008 additive revision. See fvo spec §7.6.1/§9 and errata.

title: Model unfreeze: preserve same-Part second-reference facts on Binding (ADR 0008 additive) — R4 flow-typing Class B
description: Frozen-model gap Class B (R4 spec §7.5.3 item 2): mergeBinding (internal/query/cypher/pattern.go:373-401) DISCARDS the second occurrence of an already-bound variable, so a required bare-pattern re-reference of an OPTIONAL-introduced binding in the same Part (e.g. OPTIONAL MATCH (a)-[:R]->(b) MATCH (b) RETURN b) leaves no witness in part.Bindings — the demoter cannot see it. NOT closable by the Class A group axis: the witness itself is destroyed, not merely ungrouped. Proposal per spec §7.5.4 Axis 2, two candidate shapes: (a) RequiredReferences []ClauseRef or (b) ReferencedInRequiredBarePattern bool on Binding — additive, zero-value-safe, in-protocol under ADR 0008 post-freeze revision. Capability recovered: same-Part bare-pattern demotion (regime (b) within a single Part; cross-WITH regime (b) is R5 scope and needs NO model change). PROTOCOL: model change lands in its own unfreeze PR (ADR 0008 revision), never rides a resolver-stage PR. No R4 fixture exercises this shape — the widening PR adds one. Independent of the Class A bead — owner may accept one, both, or neither. Owner decision pending.
notes: Filed at R4 close-out per merged spec §7.5.5 / §9 DoD item 9. Design detail: docs/specs/resolver-stage-r4.md §7.5.

title: Model unfreeze: OPTIONAL-clause-group axis on Binding (ADR 0008 additive) — R4 flow-typing Class A
description: Frozen-model gap Class A (R4 spec §7.5.3 items 1+3): the model does not record which bindings were introduced by the same OPTIONAL MATCH clause, so a required-edge witness inside an OPTIONAL group cannot propagate demotion to its OPTIONAL siblings, and the resolver under-demotes (e.g. demote_chained_from_required: p/r1 stay nullable although post demotes). Proposal per spec §7.5.4 Axis 1: additive OptionalGroup axis on Binding (zero-value-safe wire default) — in-protocol under ADR 0008 post-freeze revision, additive only. Capability recovered: OPTIONAL-sibling demotion — any query starting with an OPTIONAL of arity >= 2 chained through one of its members. PROTOCOL: model change lands in its own unfreeze PR (ADR 0008 revision), never rides a resolver-stage PR. On landing, a follow-up refinement widens the R4 demotion rule and updates the demote_chained_from_required golden on that widening PR. Independent of the Class B bead — owner may accept one, both, or neither. Owner decision pending.
notes: Filed at R4 close-out per merged spec §7.5.5 / §9 DoD item 9. Design detail: docs/specs/resolver-stage-r4.md §7.5.
