title: Stage 14 review follow-ups: dead intra-YIELD check, double-recorded arg refs, stale listener comment
description:
Three non-blocking findings from the Stage 14 thermo-nuclear review (all mutant-test-proven dead or stale; none affect wire or correctness):

1. call.go:139-143 — collectYieldItems intra-YIELD collision check is fully shadowed by the buildPart callByVar backstop (build.go:145-147). Disabling it fails ZERO tests. Pick one authority: delete the collectCall check, or narrow the backstop.
2. call.go:54-59 — outer ref-append loop double-records every var ref in CALL args; typeExpressionMining -> typeAtom (typing.go:324) already pushes var atoms onto curPart.refs. Disabling it fails ZERO tests (unbound-arg kill-probe still rejects). Delete the outer loop; it contradicts the save-restore convention at expr.go:159-161 / 400-402.
3. listener.go:82-83 — stale pre-Stage-13 future-tense comment ('Stage 13 will also flip it on EnterOC_Merge once MERGE lands; today MERGE stays behind ErrUnsupportedClause'). MERGE landed (Stage 13) and ErrUnsupportedClause is retired (Stage 14). Rewrite as history.

When fixing 1/2, add the missing discriminating pins if a single authority is chosen (so the survivor is mutant-detectable).
close_reason: PR #148 merged (Stage 14 dead-code removal: intra-YIELD check + double-recorded arg refs; listener.go comment rewritten as history). Linus PASS with independent kill-probes on both surviving authorities (buildPart callByVar + typeAtom refs).

title: On-disk procsig registry file format
description: Stage 14 defers the CLI-consumable on-disk format for procedure signatures. Today procsig.Registry is populated in-memory (godog step parses per-scenario declarations; real CLI callers would need to construct signatures from Go code). Design and implement a text format (YAML / JSON / .procsig) that mirrors the TCK's declaration syntax, plus a loader that constructs procsig.Registry from a file path. Follow-up to gqlc-im1.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #251. JSON on-disk registry format with strict-decoded loader (Load/Decode), canonical MarshalJSON, and Save round-trip helper. Format defensibly JSON — stdlib only, matches existing schema/query/validated precedent, extensible via omit-if-nil version field. Rejects bare null / null signatures explicitly (would otherwise silently produce the empty registry). Loader delegates semantic checks (unqualified name, duplicate column, unknown token) to existing NewRegistry so file surface and in-memory API stay lockstep. Follow-up gqlc-aqb filed for wiring into a real CLI.

title: Stage 13 spec §4.3: correct list-nested param prediction (typeAtom approves; PropertyUse recorded)
description: Reviewer SF2 from Stage 13 thermo-nuclear review: spec §4.3 lines ~903-907 predict MERGE (a {tags: [$t, $u]}) is caught by requireAllParametersApproved as ErrUnsupportedParameter. Reality: typeAtom inside the list literal marks each param approved (typing.go:335), so the widening records PropertyUse{Ref{a, tags}} on both params. Wire behavior is defensible (Use points at the right property slot, wrong nesting level; resolver reconciles) but the spec prediction is wrong. Fix: rewrite §4.3's prediction to document actual behavior and the nesting-level caveat.
close_reason: PR #142 merged (Stage 13 §4.3 corrected: list-nested params approved by typeAtom, PropertyUse{Ref{var,key}} recorded). Linus PASS with 1 NIT (expr.go:610-611 comment drift — filed as follow-up).

title: Stage 13 spec §8: retire stale posture-1 paragraph (contradicts §4.3 and shipped behavior)
description: Reviewer SF1 from Stage 13 thermo-nuclear review: spec §4.3 (lines ~875-900) describes posture 2 (record PropertyUse for params in rich inline-map values), and the implementation ships posture 2. But §8 (~line 1118) still closes with 'The current draft chooses posture 1' (defer to ExprUse via Stage 6 typer). Doc-vs-doc contradiction; §4.3 and the code win. Fix: rewrite the §8 paragraph to match shipped posture 2.
close_reason: PRs #139/#140/#143 merged; Linus PASS on each.

title: docs(spec): Stage 12 spec doc-parity follow-ups from reviewer confirmation pass
description:
Two doc-only wire-shape gaps in docs/specs/cypher-query-parser-stage-12.md found during the SF-delta confirmation pass (PR #67, reviewer CONFIRM verdict). Code truth is correct in both; the spec lags.

Gap A — §1.3 anonymous-node semantics: add one sentence naming the emergent `variables: null` outcome for anonymous-only CREATE clauses (e.g. CREATE ({prop: <literal>}) — anonymous nodes never enter curPart.bindings, so the [before..len(bindings)] delta walk yields an empty slice, marshalled as null). Goldens Temporal4_06cc612fd9c1 and Create1_2edce65e88dc confirm. Without the sentence, future readers may misread variables:null as a silent-drop bug.

Gap B — §8 SF6 disclosure golden-count phrasing: correct at 2aacf3c (~136 UNLOCK + ~24 re-keyed ≈ ~160) but SF3 (756da6e) later minted +71 more (53 create/delete + 18 Temporal4). One-line update: total Stage-12 new goldens ≈ 231; 3061 on disk at branch HEAD.
close_reason: PR #141 merged (Stage 12 doc-parity: §1.3 anon-only CREATE + §8 SF6 golden-count). Linus PASS.

title: Enrich CreateEffect to distinguish created vs pre-bound pattern variables
description: Stage 12 round-1 reviewer finding SF4: MATCH (n) CREATE (n) parses via mergeBinding, and CreateEffect.Variables comes out empty because the bindings slice doesn't grow (walk-order before..after delta is zero). Spec corrected to code truth (records only newly-created bindings). If a consumer ever needs 'which variables did this CREATE clause touch' including pre-bound ones (e.g. for typed-Create codegen over mixed MATCH+CREATE), CreateEffect needs a pre-merge variable list or a per-element created/reused axis. Blocked conceptually on a real consumer need — do not build speculatively.
notes: Re-parented from closed epic gqlc-33k to the resolver/codegen umbrella at the freeze (2026-07-03): explicitly consumer-driven (do not build speculatively) — pick up only when a consumer demonstrates the need. ADR 0008 names it a known deferred addition under the additive revision protocol.

title: Type params in EXISTS RegularQuery LIMIT/SKIP as clause-slot Uses
description: Stage 11 round-2 reviewer caveat (S5): a $param inside an EXISTS { RegularQuery-form } LIMIT or SKIP records the blanket ExprUse{TypeBool, ExprInPredicate} minted by EnterOC_ExistentialSubquery's parameter sweep, rather than the precise clause-slot Use (int, Limit/Skip). Recorded, not dropped — sound but imprecise. Cause: inner LIMIT/SKIP handlers early-return under subqueryDepth>0, so the subquery sweep is the only mining path. Fix direction: let the sweep classify params by enclosing slot, or narrowly re-enable slot handlers inside subqueries. Add spec §8 note when addressed.
notes:
Re-parented from closed epic gqlc-33k to the resolver/codegen umbrella at the freeze (2026-07-03): post-freeze Use-precision refinement, consumer-facing. ADR 0008 names it a known deferred addition under the additive revision protocol.

Autonomous agent (2026-07-11 orchestrator) claimed and stalled during golangci-lint verify with no committed work — worktree reset --hard wiped any WIP. Unclaimed for future pickup. When picking up: agent didn't leave artefacts, so start fresh from the bead brief. Grep entry points: EnterOC_ExistentialSubquery, subqueryDepth, LIMIT/SKIP handlers in internal/resolver/ and parser. Recommended fix direction (per prior orchestrator's Carmack call): approach (1) — classify params by enclosing slot inside the sweep — centralises Use-classification.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #254 (feat(resolver): precise clause-slot Uses for params in EXISTS LIMIT/SKIP). Approach 1 from bead brief: classify params by enclosing slot inside the sweep, centralising Use-classification. Post-freeze Use-precision refinement under ADR 0008 additive revision protocol. Fixtures parameter_exists_body_clause_slot_{limit,skip}.cypher pin (int, Limit)/(int, Skip) emission. Spec §8 addendum + ADR 0008 update. Recovered from stalled agent by orchestrator.

title: Dialect extension: shortestPath()/allShortestPaths() (Neo4j, outside openCypher)
description: Stage 8 review finding: MATCH p = shortestPath((a)-[*]->(b)) hits an ANTLR 'no viable alternative' parse error — the vendored openCypher grammar (internal/grammar/cypher) appears not to implement shortestPath/allShortestPaths, though openCypher considers them valid. Below the model layer entirely. Needed before the freeze claim of full pattern support; likely grammar regeneration or upstream grammar version bump, then model decision (path-typed function-like pattern). Parent epic gqlc-33k.
notes: PREMISE CORRECTED at freeze (2026-07-03): this is NOT a grammar bug. shortestPath/allShortestPaths are Neo4j dialect extensions — zero rules in the official openCypher grammar (verified against internal/grammar/cypher/Cypher.g4) and zero occurrences in the entire vendored TCK. The freeze (ADR 0008) records the posture: out of frozen scope, post-freeze additive feature under the revision protocol — expected shape is a selector axis on PathBinding, the same move EdgeHops made on EdgeBinding. Requires: grammar extension (dialect layer decision), model axis, parser lowering. De-prioritized to P3, detached from closed epic gqlc-33k.
close_reason: Won't do (owner decision, 2026-07-10). shortestPath()/allShortestPaths() are Neo4j dialect extensions with zero rules in the official openCypher grammar (verified against internal/grammar/cypher/Cypher.g4) and zero occurrences in the vendored TCK. The freeze (ADR 0008) already recorded them as out-of-scope; the owner has elected not to add the dialect layer / model axis / parser lowering that would be needed. Not superseded — dropped entirely. Reopen if a future consumer requires Neo4j-dialect parity.

Add a mustParse pin for 'MATCH (a) WITH a, count(*) AS c WHERE c > 1 RETURN a' to lock the snapshot/restore against future refactors at Layer 2.

title: Narrow bucket-3 whitelist for InvalidNumberLiteral/InvalidUnicode* details
description: Reviewer finding (Stage 6, non-blocking): InvalidNumberLiteral / InvalidUnicodeCharacter / InvalidUnicodeLiteral in the acceptance_test.go bucket-3 detail whitelist cover a mixed set — some TCK scenarios are value-level (out-of-range ints, \uH in lexed string), others parse-shape (0x with no digits, em-dash operator). Safe today (parser rejects the parse-shape subset; 0 fails) but reduces regression sensitivity to a future lexer bug labeled with those details. Narrow to the value-out-of-range subset.
close_reason: PR #145 merged (bucket-3 whitelist narrowed: removed InvalidNumberLiteral/InvalidUnicode*). Linus PASS. Zero TCK count shift; strict tightening of accept-and-defer surface.

title: Detect dead git-hook activation (core.hooksPath drift)
description: Discovered 2026-07-02: core.hooksPath in .git/config pointed at the empty .git/hooks (absolute path), silently disabling the .githooks pre-commit/pre-push gates for an unknown period — every local commit/push since ran ungated; CI caught nothing because it is the authoritative gate, but the local budget-split design (README) was dead. Origin of the override unknown (bd doctor silent on hooks; global config clean). Fixed by re-running 'just init' and verified via the hook's BRANCH_OVERRIDE test seam. Needs a cheap drift detector that runs where developers already are, since CI cannot see local git config — e.g., a warning in a frequently-run just recipe (test/lint) when core.hooksPath != .githooks, or a 'just doctor' recipe. Keep it sub-second per the hook budget philosophy.
close_reason: PRs #139/#140/#143 merged; Linus PASS on each.

title: Resolver: implicit grouping-key semantics for aggregate projections
description: Stage 3 (gqlc-npg) carries AggregateProjection with only its []Ref payload + the aggregate kind, deliberately NOT modelling implicit grouping (which non-aggregate RETURN columns form the group key). Per spec §9 (cypher-query-parser-stage-3.md) this is a resolver/Stage-4 concern entangled with WITH, and there is a real risk the []Ref-only payload proves insufficient and AggregateProjection must be reopened. This issue tracks adding grouping-key resolution once the model is frozen (gqlc-cta) and WITH lands (Stage 4, gqlc-v23).
notes: Linked to resolver stage R5 (gqlc-0mx.7) at the freeze (2026-07-03): grouping-key semantics are R5's scope; this bead carries the recorded contract (resolver-side re-parse; ContainsAggregate only as ADR-0008 escape hatch).
close_reason: Superseded by R5 spec B7 resolution (PR #101). The contract recorded here (resolver-side re-parse of ExprProjection text to detect nested aggregates) was retracted: the frozen model drops nested-aggregate structure at classification (classifyRichExpression returns plain ExprProjection; recovered text re-parses opaque, so re-parse cannot recover the aggregate bit). R5 landed the uniform-exclude posture instead: ExprProjection items are NEVER grouping keys; result-set effect is under-grouping when a Part mixes AggregateProjection with ExprProjection. Parser pin 'count in arithmetic' (parser_test.go:1319) is the explicit deferral lock. Escape hatch: Shape B ContainsAggregate model-unfreeze follow-up bead (parser-side bit set by classifyRichExpression during its own walk) filed for owner decision per ADR 0008 revision protocol.

title: Resolver: flow-type nullable bindings across clause structure
description:
Demote a binding's effective Nullable flag in the validated query when downstream clauses prove the binding must exist on every surviving row. Two regimes with different model dependencies:

(a) Edge-chain demotion (no new model structure beyond Stage 2 needed) — a non-nullable edge implies its endpoints exist, so chain 'non-nullable edge → non-nullable endpoints → demote those endpoints' nullability'. Handles the common case: OPTIONAL MATCH (a)-[:R]->(b) MATCH (b)-[:S]->(c) RETURN a, b, c — both a and b demote.

(b) Bare-pattern demotion (requires Stage 4 clause structure) — a required clause that reuses an OPTIONAL-introduced binding as a bare node/edge pattern filters NULL-rows. E.g. OPTIONAL MATCH (a)-[:R]->(b) MATCH (b) RETURN b. The model needs per-clause structure to record the second reference; Stage 4 (WITH/UNION) introduces that structure.

Per ADR 0006: the parser does not do this; the resolver does. (a) is shippable on its own after the freeze + resolver exists; (b) additionally waits on Stage 4.
notes: Linked to resolver stage R4 (gqlc-0mx.6) at the freeze (2026-07-03): flow-typing regimes a+b are R4's scope; this bead carries the ADR 0006 design context.
close_reason: Design captured and superseded by merged R4 spec (docs/specs/resolver-stage-r4.md, PR #98). Regime (a) shipped in R4 code (PR #99, 3b9d7f9). Regime (b) split per spec §7.4: cross-WITH → R5 (gqlc-0mx.7) admission-gate scope, no model change needed; same-Part → blocked on two independent frozen-model gaps (spec §7.5.3 Class A / Class B), tracked by the two unfreeze beads filed at R4 close-out. GH #21 stays open for the regime-(b) remainder. Forced past the open umbrella epic gqlc-0mx (open until R7 by design).

title: docs: Stage 1 spec §6 — make 5→3 arithmetic explicit (Metz cycle-5 non-blocking note)
description: In docs/specs/cypher-query-parser-stage-1.md §6 Layer-2 coverage gaps, the opening claim says ErrUnsupportedParameter is reachable by 5 fail-sites and the list shows 3 remaining uncovered. The intermediate isn't shown: 5 = 1 (cycle-introduced AUTHORED pin) + 1 (whole-map, corpus-covered by Match1[6] in mustReject) + 3 (uncovered). A reader counting 5-1=4 vs 3 bullets pauses unnecessarily. Insert one sentence between 'five distinct fail-sites' and 'the remaining three' that names Match1[6]'s coverage explicitly. Metz's exact suggested phrasing is in her cycle-5 diff review.
close_reason: PR #35 merged; GH #22 auto-closed

title: Stage 5 — undirected patterns
description: Support (a)-[r]-(b) patterns matching in either direction. Edge binding gains a direction-agnostic marker; resolution tries both orientations against the directed schema. May end up N/A if the schema stays directed-only — re-evaluate when reached. Per ADR 0004 §Stages.
close_reason: Stage 5 complete: undirected relationship patterns (a)-[r]-(b) and (a)<-[r]->(b) now parse. EdgeBinding gains a positive 'directed' marker (field+Directed() accessor+trailing constructor param+always-emitted JSON key); listener uses an inverted zero-value-safe 'undirected' raw field, polarity flip at toBinding. collectEdge stops rejecting left==right; directed edges canonicalise, undirected keep textual order (no flip; <--> guarded). godog read-core 371/179 -> 380/170 (+9 passed). Linus gate APPROVED. Branch stage-5-undirected pushed to origin (commits dae7fb3 prep, b474fbe model, d4fbedd unlock). Not N/A: parser is schema-agnostic, marker is load-bearing (undirected yields no single EdgeKey, resolver forms two candidate keys).

title: config spec §6.3: catalogue the explicitly-tagged garbage-int variant or hedge completeness
description: Residual from the Linus PASS on PR #266 (gqlc-8gp): 'version: !!int abc' reaches the typed decode and yields 'config: <src>: field "version": yaml: cannot decode !!str `abc` as a !!int' — the overflow row's wrapping but yaml's 'cannot decode' text, no 'unmarshal errors:' block and no line info. One variant still sits outside §6.3's claim to list every loader message. Fix options: generalize the overflow row's title to cover both yaml-wrapped int-decode failures (add the second example + a pinned test), or hedge the completeness claim with 'yaml.v3's message'. Ultra-pathological input; pure doc/test polish, no code change expected.

title: internal/config polish: line info on version-probe errors + §6.3 catalogue completeness
description:
Two non-blocking NITs from the Linus PASS verdict on PR #258 (gqlc-3w2):

1. strictInt messages omit line info (internal/config/config.go ~199-204): 'field "version" must be a YAML integer (got !!float "1.5")' carries no 'line N:' even though value.Line is available in the unmarshaler; sibling enum errors do carry it. The spec catalogue documents the line-less shape so there is no drift — pure polish. If fixed, update the §6.3 row + test substrings together (three-way consistency).

2. docs/specs/config-file-format.md §6.3 completeness micro-gap: a genuine !!int that overflows Go int (version: 9223372036854775808) produces a sane, prefixed, line-carrying yaml unmarshal error whose shape is absent from a catalogue that claims to list every loader message. Either add the row or soften the completeness claim.

Noted and deliberately NOT findings: 'version: !!int "1"' and 'version: 0x1' load — genuine !!int spellings of 1, consistent with §3.
notes: 3rd NIT (from Linus PASS on PR #264 / gqlc-suj): docs/specs/config-file-format.md §8 rationale bullet is still titled 'Required single-value enums' while its body now narrates the driver axis growing to two members — the title reads historically but is false for one of three axes. Retitle (e.g. 'Required enums, no defaults') next time the doc is touched. Cosmetic only.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #266 (squash-merged, CI green). Linus round 1: verified PASS — line-anchored version-probe errors proven value-anchored via reviewer scratch probes, overflow row deterministic under pinned yaml.v3, three-way impl/spec/test consistency verbatim, §8 retitled. Residual NIT (explicitly-tagged garbage int uncatalogued) filed as its own P4 bead.

title: R7 follow-up nits: spec scalar-casing gloss + two stale doc comments
description: Three nit-class follow-ups from the R7 cycles (PR #107 spec Linus PASS round 2; PR #108 code Linus PASS round 1). (1) docs/specs/resolver-stage-r7.md:465 quotes R2 literal goldens as {kind: "scalar", scalar: "INT"} but the goldens pin lowercase "int" (the property-arm "type": "INT" prose is correct) — one-word casing fix. (2) internal/resolver/errors.go ErrOutOfR0Scope doc comment still lists 'call bindings' among the unsupported constructs; spec §5.2 designated this comment for update when the BindingCall gate lifted (behaviorally retired in f2abd31) — remove 'call' from the 'path / unwind / call bindings' clause. (3) internal/resolver/resolve.go:121-123 comment still says the registry 'is reserved for R7. When R7 lands the CALL/YIELD handler here, drop this discard and route the registry into that handler' — contradicts the shipped §4.4 trust posture (discard STAYS; registry consulted only at parse). Rewrite to state the trust posture, matching resolver.go's updated WithRegistry doc.

title: R3 follow-up nits: same-binding self-loop fixture + spec count→weight gloss
description: Two nit-class follow-ups from the R3 code review (PR #96, Linus PASS). (1) Add a same-binding self-loop fixture — (p:Person)-[r:KNOWS]->(p) — to lock touchingSide/candidateTypes behavior; existing self_loop_directed.cypher only covers the schema-level self-loop (Source==Target label), not a single VarEndpoint on both sides. (2) docs/specs/resolver-stage-r3.md §6.4 names the type-differs property 'count' but the implementation uses 'weight' because COUNT is a GQL reserved word (GQL.g4:3319) — amend the spec line with a one-line gloss.
close_reason: PR #146 merged (R3 nits: self-loop fixture + §6.4 count→weight gloss + COUNT reserved-word gloss). Linus PASS.

title: Parser: unary-minus over a projection (RETURN -n.num) accepts as a plain ref
description: Linus review of Stage 3 unlock flagged that RETURN -n.num parses as a RefProjection to n.num rather than residual ErrUnsupportedProjection. Cause: the shared precedence-tower collapse (shape.go nonArithmeticFromAddSub) reads OC_UnaryAddOrSubtractExpression(0) without rejecting a leading '-'. Pre-existing behaviour governing Stage 0-2 too, not introduced by Stage 3, and no TCK read-core scenario exercises it — hence non-blocking. Decide whether a unary sign is arithmetic that should be residual (value lives below the type-interface boundary either way, ADR 0005) and tighten the helper if so.
close_reason: PR #36 merged: unary-signed operands now residual via GetChildCount guard in nonArithmeticFromAddSub
